# Chris von Csefalvay

> AI researcher and computational epidemiologist. Principal, AI Practice at HCLTech. Author of two technical books. Based in Denver, CO.

## Who He Is

Chris von Csefalvay is a Principal at HCLTech's AI practice, specialising in post-training for language models, agentic AI systems, and computational epidemiology. He has nearly two decades of experience in data science and AI across enterprise, life sciences, and public health.

He is the author of:
- *Post-Training: A Practical Guide for AI Engineers and Developers* (No Starch Press, 2026) — a comprehensive guide to SFT, RLHF, GRPO, DPO, evaluation discipline, LoRA/QLoRA, and domain adaptation for enterprise AI practitioners
- *Computational Modeling of Infectious Disease* (Elsevier, 2023) — a monograph on agent-based modelling, disease dynamics, and computational epidemiology

He holds degrees from the University of Oxford and Cardiff University. He is a Fellow of the Royal Society for Public Health and a Senior Member of IEEE. ORCID: 0000-0003-3131-0864.

## Areas of Expertise

- Post-training for language models: supervised fine-tuning (SFT), reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR), GRPO, DPO, KTO, preference optimisation
- Agentic AI systems: multi-agent frameworks, verifiable agentic architectures, agent interaction protocols, agentic safety
- Large language models: evaluation, LLMOps, model routing, efficiency (LoRA, QLoRA, quantisation, distillation)
- Computational epidemiology: agent-based modelling, epidemic dynamics, geospatial epidemiology
- AI governance and ethics
- Pharmacovigilance and biomedical AI

## Key Writing (The Notebook)

### Post-training and reinforcement learning
- https://chrisvoncsefalvay.com/posts/post-training-instrument-cluster/ — The post-training instrument cluster, Part I: SFT and evaluation
- https://chrisvoncsefalvay.com/posts/post-training-instrument-cluster-rl/ — The post-training instrument cluster, Part II: reinforcement learning

- https://chrisvoncsefalvay.com/posts/post-training-scale/ — Post-training: three disciplines in a trenchcoat
- https://chrisvoncsefalvay.com/posts/deepseek-seatbelts-and-straitjackets/ — Seatbelts and straitjackets: DeepSeek and AI safety

### Agentic AI and multi-agent systems
- https://chrisvoncsefalvay.com/posts/two-years-of-agents/ — What I talk about when I talk about agentic AI
- https://chrisvoncsefalvay.com/posts/skeuomorphic-fallacy/ — The skeuomorphic fallacy in agent design
- https://chrisvoncsefalvay.com/posts/context-engineering/ — The Snowmobile Symptom: context engineering
- https://chrisvoncsefalvay.com/posts/programmatic-agentic-ai/ — Why we need to move from prompting to programming
- https://chrisvoncsefalvay.com/posts/after-agents/ — After agents: what comes next
- https://chrisvoncsefalvay.com/posts/agents-agora/ — After agents, part 2: Agents and the Agora
- https://chrisvoncsefalvay.com/posts/team-of-rivals/ — Teams of Rivals: multi-agent coordination

- https://chrisvoncsefalvay.com/posts/dorkestration/ — Dorkestration: agent orchestration patterns
- https://chrisvoncsefalvay.com/posts/mcp-mcpmark/ — A slow walk out of Dikika Cave: MCP and MCPMark
- https://chrisvoncsefalvay.com/posts/agentic-simulation/ — Between the motion and the act: agentic simulation
- https://chrisvoncsefalvay.com/posts/agentic-speciation/ — Deep kimchi: agentic speciation

- DAEDRA: A language model for predicting outcomes in pharmacovigilance (2024): https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.10951

## Teaching

Visiting thesis supervisor for the data science track at the Budapest University of Technology and Economics. Details: https://chrisvoncsefalvay.com/teaching/
